sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.6 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: A group$$is said to have Property$P_{$} if, for any finite subset$F$of$\{1\},$there exists an element$y_0$of infinite order such that, for each$x F$, the canonical epimorphism from the free product$x * y_0$onto the subgroup$x , y_0$of$$generated by$x$and$y_0$is an isomorphism. For$n \{2,\,3, \},$does$PSL_n({ Z} )$have Property$P_{ }$? More generally, if$$is a lattice in a connected real Lie group$...
